what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment in the design-assets channel, linking an images-type attachment to a target image URI and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
standouts: Target URI uses an image:// scheme and ends with a parenthetical number (54)
The file is purely a minimal metadata record with no explanatory prose
It belongs to the design-assets channel and is typed as images
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
Timestamp follows ISO 8601 with explicit timezone
The body after the frontmatter is empty
Target URI contains spaces and parentheses rather than percent-encoded characters
concerns: Target URI contains unescaped spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or resolution issues
No filename, MIME type, dimensions, or checksum for the image
attachment_type uses plural 'images' instead of a singular type label
notes: A minimal ledger entry mapping a design-assets channel attachment to an image URI with a numeric suffix.
questions: What does the '(54)' suffix in the target URI represent—version, index, duplicate, or arbitrary label?
What is the actual filename or storage path of the referenced image?
Is the image:// URI scheme resolved by an internal asset service?
Who added this attachment and what triggered its creation?
intention_signals: Track an image asset attached to the design-assets channel by storing its attachment id, type, target URI, and creation timestamp.
novelty: The target URI includes a literal space and a parenthetical number, which is an uncommon shape for a clean asset reference.
contribution: It preserves the exact attachment identity, channel, type, target URI, and timestamp for an image asset, supporting traceability of when a design-assets image attachment was recorded.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel attachment ledger record for the design-assets channel, identifying an images-type attachment with id att-c29d611c that targets image://design-assets/icons/image (54) and recording an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to register an image asset attachment in the design-assets channel for later reference or retrieval.
